# Axiarch (AX-ee-ark)

> Constitution-Driven AI Agent Governance Framework

Axiarch is an open-source governance framework that mitigates quality drift,
hallucination, and uncontrolled behavior in AI-assisted software development.
It enforces minimum engineering standards through a multi-layer constitutional
architecture, ensuring consistent output quality regardless of the AI agent
or operator skill level.

## What Problem Does Axiarch Solve?

Without governance, AI agents suffer from:
- **Context amnesia**: Architectural decisions are forgotten between sessions
- **Operator-dependent quality**: Output quality varies with instruction precision
- **Vibe coding drift**: Code that looks correct but silently violates patterns
- **Knowledge evaporation**: Hard-won lessons are lost and re-discovered repeatedly

Axiarch mitigates these risks by enforcing a firm quality floor and autonomous context loading through constitutional architecture.

## Core Architecture (Three-Layer Governance)

- **Layer 1: Universal (Immutable Constitution)**
  - `AGENTS.md` — Supreme law (AI Behavior Protocols). Read by: Google Antigravity, Cursor, Claude Code, etc.
  - `axiarch-rules/universal/` — 38 immutable rule files (2,500+ engineering standards across Security, API, SRE, FinOps...).
- **Layer 2: Blueprint (Mutable Project State)**
  - `axiarch-rules/blueprint/` — Evolving project memory. Project overview, feature specs, and continuously accumulated lessons log.
- **Layer 3: Prompts (Optional Execution Framework)**
  - `axiarch-prompts/` — Reusable audit/QA prompt templates to forcefully apply Layer 1/2 rules into action.

## Key Protocols

- **LOADING_PROTOCOL.md**: 5-step rule hierarchy loading sequence
- **CRYSTALLIZATION_PROTOCOL.md**: Automatic lesson-to-rule conversion
- **Boot Sequence Protocol**: Mitigates hallucination risk by enforcing rules-first behavior
- **Blueprint First**: Specs required before code for major changes
- **Deployment Ban Protocol**: No `git push` without explicit user approval

## Quick Start

```bash
# Option 1: Interactive setup script (recommended)
curl -sSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/hiroyuki-miyauchi/axiarch/main/init.sh | bash

# Option 2: Manual copy
cp AGENTS.md /path/to/your/project/
cp -r axiarch-rules /path/to/your/project/
```

## Compatibility

| Agent | Status |
|:------|:-------|
| Google Antigravity | ✅ Verified — battle-tested in production |
| Cursor | ⚠️ Expected to work (reads AGENTS.md) |
| Claude Code | ⚠️ Expected to work (symlink AGENTS.md → CLAUDE.md) |
| GitHub Copilot | ⚠️ Expected to work (reads AGENTS.md) |
| Windsurf / Aider / Zed | ⚠️ Expected to work |

## Language Support

Fully bilingual: Japanese (authoritative) and English (symmetric counterpart).
All 38 universal rules and 4 blueprint templates are available in both languages.
The optional prompt library (`axiarch-prompts/`) is also fully bilingual (JA/EN).
